principle: Room-scale floor, ceiling, and wall identity comes from Hapax compositor/drift material contracts; DarkPlaces BSP supplies sealed walkable surfaces, collision, lighting, and final spatial rendering.

information_surface_contract.principle: Textures are permitted only when they operate as information surfaces. They may carry orientation, state, rhythm, provenance, liveness, or homage grammar; they may not simulate identifiable scenic materials such as stone, wood, metal, brick, ceiling tile, or Quake-native texture identity.
failure_predicates: A room texture can be named as a real-world material before it can be named as an information role.
A texture repeats at a scale that reads as decorative tiling rather than spatial encoding.
A surface contains copied homage/source pixels instead of clean-room procedural homage grammar.
A texture has no declared relationship to ward placement, path orientation, source state, or drift/compositor behavior.
Texture detail competes with media legibility from the intended inspection station.

surfaces.purpose: walkable garden floor carrying low-contrast path-rhythm signal so the room remains legible while ward/media/lightfield behavior stays primary

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP floor brush texture cmp_floor as dark collision and orientation receiver
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax compositor/drift material profile generates a non-scenic floor signal field baked into WAD; live expression remains entity/compositor behavior
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed texture now; BGRA live receiver when slot registry expands
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static compositor-derived substrate with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and postprocess
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale field, not media aspect; only low-contrast path rhythm and orientation marks are admissible
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns floor material grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns walkable collision and spatial lighting

surfaces.purpose: overhead dark occlusion receiver with faint canopy signal so the room has visible height without becoming Quake architecture

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP ceiling brush texture cmp_ceil as dark occlusion and canopy receiver
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax compositor/drift material profile generates a non-scenic ceiling signal field baked into WAD; live expression remains entity/compositor behavior
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed texture now; BGRA live receiver when slot registry expands
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static compositor-derived substrate with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and postprocess
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale field, not media aspect; faint overhead linework may orient the walker but must not compete with wards
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns ceiling material grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns occlusion and spatial lighting

surfaces.purpose: neutral no-front enclosure carrying dark boundary signal without Quake scenic texture identity or static mounting structure

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP wall brush texture cmp_wall as dark sealing and boundary receiver
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax compositor/drift material profile generates a non-scenic wall boundary field baked into WAD; live expression remains entity/compositor behavior
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed texture now; BGRA live receiver when slot registry expands
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static compositor-derived substrate with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and postprocess
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale field, not media aspect; wall grid/support marks are forbidden until they are explicit ward entities
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns wall material grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns room sealing and spatial lighting
